questions.question: A CI/CD pipeline has separate stages: Build → Test → Deploy. The Test stage fails. What happens to Deploy?
options: Deploy runs anyway since stages are independent
Deploy is skipped because the pipeline stops on failure
Deploy runs but with a warning flag
The entire pipeline restarts from Build

questions.question: In the Waterfall model, testing revealed a major flaw in the original requirements. Why is this especially costly?
options: Testing tools are expensive to license
You must go back through all completed phases to fix it
Waterfall does not allow any testing at all
The development team has already been disbanded

questions.question: In Agile, delivering a working prototype every sprint is more valuable than completing all documentation first.
An EC2 instance is running but your website returns 'connection timed out'. Which should you check FIRST?
options: Whether the EC2 instance type is powerful enough
The Security Group inbound rules for port 80/443
If AWS is having a global outage
The color scheme of your website

questions.question: You uploaded a 2GB video to S3. A week later you upload a new version with the same filename. What happens to the old file?
options: Both versions are kept automatically
The old file is overwritten (unless versioning is enabled)
S3 rejects the upload because the file already exists
The old file is moved to a backup bucket

questions.question: The Linux command to search for the word 'error' inside all .log files is: _____ 'error' *.log
A GitHub Actions workflow triggers 'on: push' to main. A developer pushes to a feature branch. Does the workflow run?
options: Yes, 'on: push' triggers on all branches
No, it only triggers on pushes to main
Yes, but it runs with reduced permissions
It depends on the developer's role in the repo

questions.question: The SDLC is a fixed set of 5 steps that every company must follow in exact order.
You ran 'git commit' but forgot to add a file. The commit is already made. What is the BEST way to include the missing file?
options: Delete the repository and start over
Run 'git add <file>' then 'git commit --amend'
Push the commit and fix it in the next sprint
Use 'git revert' to undo all changes

questions.question: Jest reports '4 tests passed, 1 snapshot failed'. What does a snapshot failure mean?
options: The test took too long to execute
The component's rendered output changed compared to the stored snapshot
Jest could not find the test file
A syntax error exists in the test

questions.question: SSH uses port 22 by default. If a firewall blocks all ports except 80, 443, and 8080, how many of these ports can SSH use without its default?
A cron expression '0 9 * * 1' runs a job. When does it execute?
options: Every day at 9 AM
Every Monday at 9 AM
Every January 9th
Every 9 minutes on Monday

questions.question: Running 'chmod +x script.sh' allows anyone to read the script but only the owner to execute it.
In YAML, which of these is INVALID syntax?
options: name: "John Doe"
items:\n  - apple\n  - banana
count: 42
name: John Doe\n  age: 25

questions.question: GitHub Secrets are masked in logs. But a developer writes: 'echo $SECRET | base64'. Is the secret still protected?
options: Yes, GitHub masks all transformations of secrets
No, the base64-encoded version is not recognized and will be printed in plain text
GitHub will automatically delete the workflow run
base64 encoding is impossible in GitHub Actions

questions.question: To stage ALL modified and new files for a git commit, you run: git add _____
A deployment pipeline automates the path from code to production. Which stage is LEAST likely to be part of it?
options: Running automated tests
Building the application artifact
Writing the application source code
Deploying to a staging environment

questions.question: The 'pre-commit' git hook can prevent a developer from committing code that fails linting checks.
You SSH into a server and run a long process. You close your laptop and the SSH session disconnects. What happens to the process?
options: It keeps running forever in the background
It receives SIGHUP and terminates (unless run with nohup/tmux/screen)
SSH automatically reconnects and resumes the process
The server reboots to clean up orphaned processes

questions.question: In Agile, the team consistently fails to complete sprint goals. What is the BEST response?
options: Switch to Waterfall for more structure
Reduce the scope of upcoming sprints based on actual velocity
Extend the sprint from 2 weeks to 2 months
Remove testing from the sprint to save time
